description: sherpa-node-canvas.js — Pan/zoom canvas hosting sherpa-node children. Owns: - viewport {x, y, zoom}: pan via middle-mouse OR space+left-drag, zoom via wheel (cursor-anchored, clamp 0.1–2.5). - edges:  Array<{ from:{nodeId,portName}, to:{nodeId,portName}, control?:boolean }> rendered as bezier curves. - drag-to-connect: pointerdown on a slotted <sherpa-node-socket> starts an edge drag; pointerup over another socket completes it. - drag-to-redirect: pointerdown on an input socket that already has edge(s) picks up the end nearest the click. Drop on empty space deletes; drop on another valid socket updates. - hover/select state for edges (token-coloured), with hit-testing via 32-segment bezier polyline + 6 px perpendicular threshold. - multi-input sockets: connection count is mirrored to data-connection-count on the socket; endpoints are spaced evenly across the socket's vertical extent. - crosshair grid drawn beneath edges.
